uniqueFacts: Custer County was originally named G County when created in 1892 from Cheyenne-Arapaho reservation lands, then renamed in 1896 after General George Armstrong Custer, who fought the Southern Cheyenne at the Battle of the Washita about 20 miles west in Roger Mills County.

The county is home to Southwestern Oklahoma State University (SWOSU), founded in 1901 and opened in Weatherford in 1903. The university's 1909 Science Building is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.

Weatherford's Stafford Air & Space Museum is a Smithsonian Affiliate housing over 63,000 sq. ft. of exhibits, including actual flown spacecraft from the Gemini, Apollo, and Space Shuttle programs, honoring hometown astronaut Lt. General Thomas P. Stafford.

The Washita National Wildlife Refuge, established in 1961, encompasses 8,075 acres in the county and serves as a wintering home for over 80,000 geese and other migratory waterfowl along the Central Flyway.

Custer County contains the city of Clinton, known as the 'Hub City of Western Oklahoma,' which grew rapidly in 1903 when approximately 100 buildings were moved from the nearby town of Parkersburg after railroad lines were built through Clinton.
processServingNotes: All process serving in Custer County is overseen by the Court Clerk's office at the Custer County Courthouse, 675 B Street, Arapaho, OK 73620. The Court Clerk is Staci Hunter, phone 580-323-3233.

Custer County is part of Oklahoma's 2nd Judicial District (Beckham, Custer, Ellis, Roger Mills, Washita counties). Case numbers follow the format CF-YYYY-NNNN for criminal felonies, CM-YYYY-NNNN for misdemeanors, and CJ-YYYY-NNNN for civil cases.

Under Oklahoma law, process servers must be licensed and bonded. Service may be performed by sheriff's deputies, licensed private process servers, or persons specially appointed by the court. Personal service, substituted service, and service by publication are all recognized methods.
heroLead: Nestled in west-central Oklahoma's Gypsum Hills, Custer County blends rich Cheyenne-Arapaho heritage with a thriving modern economy anchored by Southwestern Oklahoma State University, major food processing, and aerospace history at the Stafford Air & Space Museum. Whether your legal needs take you to Weatherford, Clinton, or the county seat of Arapaho, professional process serving ensures your documents reach their destination efficiently. Contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832 or visit our /contact page to get started.

faqs.question: Where is the Custer County Courthouse located, and what are the office hours?
faqs.answer: The Custer County Courthouse is located at 675 B Street in Arapaho, Oklahoma 73620. The Court Clerk's office, where process serving documents are filed, is open Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM, remaining open through the lunch hour. The Court Clerk is Staci Hunter and can be reached at 580-323-3233. For process serving assistance in Custer County, contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.

faqs.question: What towns and cities are located within Custer County, Oklahoma?
faqs.answer: The largest city in Custer County is Weatherford, home to Southwestern Oklahoma State University. Other incorporated communities include Clinton (part of which extends into Washita County), Thomas, Arapaho (the county seat), Custer City, Butler, and Hammon (part of which extends into Roger Mills County). Each municipality has its own municipal court for local ordinance violations, while the Custer County District Court in Arapaho handles all felony, civil, family, and probate matters.

faqs.question: Is Custer County within any tribal jurisdiction, and how does that affect legal proceedings?
faqs.answer: Yes, Custer County lies within the tribal jurisdictional area of the Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribes. The tribes' jurisdictional boundaries encompass Beckham, Blaine, Canadian, Custer, Dewey, Ellis, Kingfisher, Roger Mills, and Washita Counties. The Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribes operate their own tribal court system and provide legal aid programs that represent tribal members in tribal court and in courts throughout the jurisdictional area. Process servers working in Custer County should be aware that certain legal matters involving enrolled tribal members may fall under tribal court jurisdiction. For professional process serving guidance, contact Just Legal Solutions.

faqs.question: What major highways and transportation corridors run through Custer County?
faqs.answer: Interstate 40, one of the nation's primary east-west interstate highways, passes directly through Clinton and Weatherford. U.S. Highway 183 runs north-south through the county, connecting towns including Buffalo (Dewey County) to Clinton and onward to Vernon, Texas. Historic Route 66 also follows Main Street through Weatherford. These major transportation routes make Custer County an important logistics corridor in western Oklahoma.

faqs.question: What is the population and economic base of Custer County?
faqs.answer: As of 2024, Custer County has an estimated population of approximately 28,266 residents. The county's economy is anchored by higher education (Southwestern Oklahoma State University), healthcare (Weatherford Regional Hospital), food processing (Bar-S Foods, Mars Petcare), agriculture, and gaming/tourism (Lucky Star Casinos). The county covers approximately 1,002 square miles, making it the 33rd most populous of Oklahoma's 77 counties. For business and legal document service needs, call Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.

faqs.question: How do I look up court cases in Custer County, and what judicial district is it in?
faqs.answer: Custer County is part of Oklahoma's 2nd Judicial District, which also includes Beckham, Ellis, Roger Mills, and Washita Counties. Court cases can be searched online through the Oklahoma State Courts Network (OSCN) at oscn.net using the database key 'custer.' Case numbers follow the standard Oklahoma format: CF-YYYY-NNNN for criminal felonies, CM-YYYY-NNNN for misdemeanors, CJ-YYYY-NNNN for civil cases over $10,000, and CD-YYYY-NNNN for domestic/family matters. The current District Judge for Custer County is the Honorable Donna Dirickson, appointed in 2024. For professional process serving and court document assistance, contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.
